name: Ozena, the Suffering and Desperate - Part Two: The Red Village

text.content:
Ozena, the Suffering and Desperate

Part Two: The Red Village

Continuing the Three-Part Campaign

Location: Black Forest, South Germany - The Village of Rot

Duration: 3-4 Sessions

What Happened in Part One

The Hunters encountered OZena (Dorothea Gray) and her companions as they hunted Margareta Westen, the Good Witch of the Yellow Village. Whether Margareta survived or not, OZena obtained another piece of the Compass Rose and now sets her sights on Helga, the Red Witch who rules the southern village of Rot.

Unlike Margareta's haven for outcasts, Rot is a much darker place—a village where blood magic reigns and strange creatures serve a witch who has built a cult around herself.

Order Support

Before the Hunters depart for Rot, the Order provides them with a powerful support item:

Mirror of a Thousand Wishes

Treasures of the Order of the Hidden Rose

This ancient mirror allows the viewer to see fragments of the past and possible futures. Once per Scenario, a Hunter can gaze into the mirror and ask one question about:

  • Something that happened in the past at their current location
  • A possible outcome of a specific course of action
  • The location of a person or object they seek

The mirror shows cryptic visions rather than clear answers, but provides 3 Additional Successes on the next Check related to the revealed information.

Limitation: The mirror cannot predict the actions of Dark Presences or reveal information about the One Who Plots in the Shadows directly.

The Story So Far

OZena has now murdered two witches—Johanne in Schiltach and (possibly) Margareta in Gelb—and collected two pieces of the Compass Rose. Her desperation grows with each killing, and her companions are increasingly troubled by what they're becoming.

Now OZena turns south to Rot, where Helga the Red Witch awaits. But this time, something has changed: Helga knows OZena is coming, and she's prepared a trap.

Dorothea's Capture

When the Hunters arrive in the region around Rot, they discover disturbing news: OZena attempted to infiltrate the village alone but was captured by Helga's servants. She is being held prisoner, and Helga has announced that on the next full moon (three days away), she will perform a blood sacrifice—offering OZena's life to strengthen her own magic.

OZena's three companions—Lowe, Hirnloser, and Dose—are desperate to rescue her but lack the power to assault Helga's stronghold directly. They may approach the Hunters for help, or the Hunters may choose to intervene on their own.

The Moral Dilemma

This presents a complex choice:

  • OZena is a murderer who has killed (possibly) two witches
  • But she's also a victim—manipulated and suffering
  • Helga is preparing to commit murder through ritual sacrifice
  • But she's also defending herself against an assassin
  • Do the Hunters save OZena, or let Helga's justice be done?

FIRST SCENE - Towards the Village of Rot

The journey to Rot takes the Hunters through increasingly dark and twisted forest. The trees seem almost alive, their branches reaching out like grasping hands. Strange red flowers bloom everywhere, and the air smells of copper—like blood.

The Animated Forest

Suddenly, the forest itself attacks! Trees animate, vines lash out, and the Hunters must make a Check against OL Medium 5 to navigate through without becoming entangled.

If they fail, they're caught in the animated vegetation and must fight their way free. The forest doesn't truly want to kill them—it's a warning system, trapping intruders until Helga's servants arrive.

Wolfgang the Druid

A man in druidic robes appears, holding a wooden staff. This is Wolfgang, who introduces himself as Helga's druid and guardian of the forest. He demands to know the Hunters' business.

If questioned, Wolfgang seems nervous and afraid. The truth, if the Hunters investigate:

  • Wolfgang isn't a real druid—he's a pharmacist from a nearby town
  • Helga kidnapped him and his knowledge of herbs/medicines
  • He's forced to serve her or she'll kill his family
  • He maintains the forest trap by sprinkling blood at the roots

Wolfgang can be convinced to help the Hunters if they promise to free him from Helga's control. He knows the safe paths through the forest and can provide information about the village layout.

Finding the Clue

As the Hunters navigate the forest, they discover a clue: elderberry plants growing wild. Wolfgang reacts with surprise—he needs elderberry for a potion Helga demands, but he can never find enough.

If the Hunters gather elderberry and ask Wolfgang about it, he reveals:

  • Helga is brewing something called the Infusion of Purity
  • It requires burdock, nettle, hyssop, and elderberry
  • The potion is supposed to break blood magic curses
  • But Helga won't let him include elderberry—she says it's too rare

This is important: a complete Infusion of Purity can break Helga's blood magic control!

NPCs

Wolfgang, the enslaved pharmacist
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)

Descriptor: I'm a simple pharmacist forced to pretend to be a druid. Helga threatens my family if I don't obey. I hate what I've become, but I don't know how to escape.

SECOND SCENE - The Greenhouse

On the outskirts of Rot stands a large greenhouse made of iron and glass—an anachronistic structure that seems out of place in 1780. This is where Wolfgang brews potions and tends to Helga's medicinal plants.

The greenhouse is guarded by one of Helga's most terrifying servants: Manycorpses.

Manycorpses

This creature appears as multiple human bodies fused together into one horrifying whole—the result of Helga's blood magic experiments on lovers who begged her to never be separated. She granted their wish in the cruelest way possible.

Manycorpses guards the greenhouse and attacks intruders on sight. It's incredibly dangerous—it can act twice per Exchange and moves along walls and ceilings like a spider.

Inside the Greenhouse

If the Hunters defeat or bypass Manycorpses, they find:

  • Rows of plants growing in carefully tended beds
  • Glass vials filled with blood (used as fertilizer)
  • Wolfgang's notes on the Infusion of Purity
  • Burdock, nettle, and hyssop growing abundantly
  • No elderberry anywhere

The notes reveal the complete recipe:

"To break blood magic's hold, combine burdock (cleansing), nettle (protection), hyssop (purification), and elderberry (freedom). Brew under a full moon. The blood-bound will be freed, but the blood-binder will age terribly in moments."

The Hunters can prepare the Infusion if they have elderberry from Scene One!

NPCs

Manycorpses
OL Hard 7 – Minor NPC (4 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am many bodies merged into one through blood magic. I was lovers who begged to never be separated. Now we are together forever, twisted and wrong, serving the witch who did this to us.

Gift - Aberration

Manycorpses is a magical aberration with two abilities:

Act Twice: Manycorpses can perform two actions per Exchange instead of one. It can attack different targets or perform complex maneuvers.

Wall-Walking: Manycorpses can move along walls and ceilings as easily as floors. It can attack from unexpected angles, giving defenders 1 Penalty on their Defense Checks.

THIRD SCENE - The Dolls' Court

The village of Rot itself is a disturbing sight. The buildings are dilapidated and stained red—whether from paint or dried blood, it's hard to tell. The villagers move like puppets on strings, their eyes glazed and distant.

In the village square, a bizarre figure holds court: the Porcelain Princess.

The Porcelain Princess

She appears as a beautiful woman wearing an elaborate porcelain mask and elegant gown. But beneath the mask, her face is hideously disfigured by disease. She manages Helga's cult, organizing the blood rituals and maintaining order in Rot.

The Princess is surrounded by her Porcelain Dolls—twelve figures in porcelain masks, each carrying a different weapon. They stand perfectly still until commanded to move.

The Confrontation

The Princess knows why the Hunters have come. She offers them a deal:

  • Leave now and Helga will spare OZena's life (lie)
  • Join the cult and receive Helga's blessing (eternal servitude)
  • Try to rescue OZena and die in the attempt

If the Hunters refuse, the Princess signals her Dolls to attack. Combat in the square is complicated by the mind-controlled villagers who get in the way.

The Infusion Gambit

If the Hunters have prepared the Infusion of Purity, they can use it here:

  • Splashing it on villagers frees them from blood magic control
  • Freed villagers panic and flee or turn on the Dolls
  • The Princess reacts with fear—she knows what the Infusion does

Finding the Key

If the Hunters defeat the Princess, they find a key to Helga's mansion and a vial of blood that glows faintly. The Princess gasps: "You fools... you've doomed her. Helga will drink OZena's blood before you reach her!"

NPCs

Porcelain Princess
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I was a noblewoman disfigured by disease. Society rejected me, so I embraced Helga's offer—power in exchange for service. Now I manage her cult and keep the villagers in line.

Porcelain Dolls (x12)
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPCs (1 Wound each)

Descriptor: We are the blood-bound servants, our wills not our own.

Gift - Armed Guard

The Porcelain Dolls carry six different weapon types. Roll 1d6 to determine each Doll's weapon:

  1. Spear: Can attack from 10 feet away
  2. Double Weapon: Can attack twice in one Exchange
  3. Great Blade: Inflicts 1 additional Wound on successful hit
  4. Whip: Can disarm opponents
  5. Soaked Weapon: Poisoned blade, victim takes 1 Wound at start of next Exchange
  6. Shield: Has +1 Defense against physical attacks

FOURTH SCENE - The Red Witch

Helga's mansion stands at the center of Rot—a three-story building painted entirely red. Strange symbols are carved into every surface, and the windows glow with crimson light.

Inside, the mansion is opulent but disturbing. Every room features artwork depicting blood rituals, and the air reeks of copper. The walls seem to pulse like living tissue.

Finding OZena

The Hunters navigate through the mansion, facing blood magic traps and mind-controlled servants, until they reach the top floor. Here they find a large chamber set up for ritual sacrifice:

  • A stone altar in the center, carved with runes
  • Dorothea (OZena) bound and gagged on the altar
  • Candles surrounding her, blood pooled beneath
  • Helga standing over her, preparing the ritual

Helga the Red Witch

Helga is a striking woman who appears to be in her 40s, with long red hair and eyes that seem to glow. She wears elaborate red robes and is covered in blood (whether her own or others' is unclear).

When the Hunters arrive, Helga pauses her ritual:

"So, the Order sends its lapdogs. Do you know what this woman has done? She murdered Johanne and Margareta—good women who harmed no one. Now she pays the price in blood."

The Final Battle

Helga won't negotiate unless:

  • The Hunters have evidence of Henkle's manipulation
  • They use the Infusion of Purity to break her blood magic
  • They appeal to her original purpose as a guardian of the Compass Rose

If combat begins, Helga is incredibly powerful:

  • She can control blood—making wounds bleed more, turning blood into weapons
  • She can take control of anyone with an open wound
  • If killed, she regenerates 1 Wound immediately by draining blood from nearby sources
  • She visibly ages when her blood magic is broken or depleted

Breaking the Curse

If the Hunters use the complete Infusion of Purity (with elderberry), they can:

  • Throw it on Helga to break her blood magic powers
  • Free OZena and all blood-controlled servants
  • Reveal Helga's true age—she's ancient, kept young by blood magic

When her magic fails, Helga ages rapidly, becoming a withered old woman. She may beg for mercy or curse the Hunters with her dying breath.

Helga, The Red Witch

OL Hard 7 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am a blood witch, centuries old but kept young by my magic. I guard the southern piece of the Compass Rose and punish those who threaten my domain. My power comes from blood willingly given—or forcibly taken.

Agenda: Protect my piece of the Compass Rose / Maintain my cult and power base / Punish OZena for murdering my sister witches / Stay young and powerful forever.

Gift - Blood Magic

Helga is a master of blood magic with three primary abilities:

Blood Control: Helga can manipulate any blood she can see. She can make wounds bleed more profusely (inflicting 1 additional Wound), turn spilled blood into sharp projectiles, or create barriers of hardened blood.

Blood Puppet: Once per Scene, Helga can take control of any creature with an open wound within 30 feet. The victim must make a Check against her Opposition Level to resist. If they fail, they're under her control for one Exchange.

Blood Healing: Helga can drain blood from nearby sources (servants, animals, pooled blood) to heal herself. She can restore 1 Wound by draining one human servant completely.

Gift - Blood Witch

Helga has sustained her life for centuries through blood magic. When she would be reduced to 0 Wounds, she instead:

  • Drains blood from all nearby sources
  • Regenerates 1 Wound immediately
  • Ages visibly (gains wrinkles, gray hair, etc.)
  • Can do this once per Scene

When killed permanently, Helga rapidly ages centuries in moments, crumbling to dust.

Vulnerability

The complete Infusion of Purity (with elderberry) negates Helga's blood magic for one Scene when splashed on her. Without her magic, she's just an ordinary (very old) woman.

Roleplaying Helga

  • Voice: Commanding and imperious, with an ancient accent
  • Mannerisms: Gestures dramatically, blood swirls around her when angry
  • Motivation: Initially defensive (protecting against OZena), but corrupted by centuries of power
  • Truth: She started as a guardian like Margareta but became a tyrant
  • Redemption: If spared and freed from blood magic's corruption, she may reveal important truths

The Hammerheads

OL Medium 5 – Minor NPCs (1 Wound)

Descriptor: We are Helga's elite guards, warriors who wear massive hammer-shaped helmets that protect our heads and torsos.

Gift - Hammerhead Helmet

The Hammerheads wear specially designed helmets that extend down to cover their heads and chests completely. These provide:

  • Head/Chest Immunity: Attacks targeting the head or chest automatically miss or deal no damage
  • Vulnerable Lower Body: Attackers can target the lower body (legs, abdomen), but this requires 1 Penalty on the attack Check

The only way to damage a Hammerhead is to specifically aim low, accepting the Penalty, or use magic/area attacks that don't require precise targeting.

Tactics

Hammerheads work in pairs:

  • One engages directly while the other flanks
  • They use their helmets to headbutt opponents
  • They protect Helga fanatically
  • If the Infusion breaks Helga's control, they stop fighting and flee

Epilogue: The Road to Smaragd

What the Hunters Learn

After Helga is defeated or negotiated with, the Hunters can learn important information:

From Helga (if she survives):

  • The Compass Rose was split by the four witches to prevent misuse
  • Dr. Norman Henkle has taken over Smaragd at the center of the forest
  • Henkle murdered or drove out Griselda, the fourth witch
  • He's been manipulating OZena from the beginning
  • The incomplete Compass Rose is incredibly dangerous
  • Henkle has fortified Smaragd and is preparing something terrible

From OZena (if rescued):

  • She realizes she's been manipulated and used
  • Henkle promised her a cure but has been lying all along
  • She's killed two innocent women for nothing
  • She wants to make amends but doesn't know how
  • Her companions (Lowe, Hirnloser, Dose) are questioning everything

The Choice

The Hunters must decide what to do with OZena:

  • Turn her over to authorities: She'll be executed for murder, but justice will be served
  • Take her prisoner: The Order can judge her actions
  • Offer redemption: She can help stop Henkle and save her companions
  • Execute her: She's a murderer and doesn't deserve mercy

Dorothea's Transformation

If offered redemption, Dorothea undergoes a dramatic change:

  • She removes her silver mask, accepting her disfigurement
  • She vows to stop Henkle and free her companions from his manipulation
  • She reveals that Henkle has been using time-displacement to conduct experiments
  • She becomes a potential ally for Part Three

Foreshadowing Part Three

The final piece of the Compass Rose is in Smaragd, at the center of the Black Forest. But Henkle has transformed the village into a fortress filled with strange creatures born from twisted wishes granted by a magical wishing well.

Rumors from refugees describe:

  • Henkle as a wizard in a castle made of oak
  • A hot-air balloon being constructed
  • Strange creatures with powers born from granted wishes
  • Canine Tigers that guard the approaches
  • Lowe, Hirnloser, and Dose preparing a final defense

The Hunters must prepare for their most dangerous challenge yet: confronting Dr. Henkle in Smaragd and preventing him from activating the incomplete Compass Rose.

Scenario Events

  • If Wolfgang is killed by the Hunters: The forest becomes even more hostile. Without his maintenance, the blood-fed plants grow wild and attack indiscriminately.
  • If the Hunters use the Infusion of Purity on villagers: Freed villagers provide information about Helga's ritual chamber and may create distractions.
  • If Manycorpses is killed: It speaks with multiple voices as it dies: "Thank you... we can finally be... separate... at last..."
  • If the Porcelain Princess is spared: She removes her mask and reveals her disfigured face, explaining how Helga promised her beauty in exchange for service. She can be convinced to help.
  • If Helga is killed with blood magic still active: Her death triggers a massive blood detonation, forcing everyone in the mansion to make a Defense Check or take 1 Wound.
  • If the Hunters rescue OZena: She's weak and traumatized but grateful. She finally admits that Henkle has been lying to her all along.
  • If OZena is left to die: Lowe, Hirnloser, and Dose turn hostile and will appear as enemies in Part Three. They view the Hunters as murderers no better than Henkle.
  • If the Hunters obtain Helga's piece of the Compass Rose: It resembles a red compass needle made of bloodstone. It pulses like a heartbeat and always points toward Smaragd.
  • If Dorothea is offered redemption: She becomes an ally for Part Three, providing insider knowledge of Henkle's plans and the layout of Smaragd.

flags.setting: Black Forest, Germany - 1780 - The Village of Rot
